# SC-10: Software Composition Analysis (SCA) & SBOM Generation

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Scan all third-party dependencies — application libraries, packages, container base images, and AI model packages — against CVE databases (NVD, OSV) at every commit. Generate and maintain a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) in standard format (CycloneDX or SPDX). Block commits that introduce critical/high-severity known vulnerabilities without documented exception. Publish SBOM as a release artefact.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 3 | Coding & Implementation | Mandatory | *SCA runs at commit time to catch vulnerable dependencies before they reach testing. SBOM generation must capture the full dependency tree including transitive dependencies.*

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Automated with human exception management |
| **Agent does** | Execute SCA scanner (OWASP Dependency-Check, Trivy, Snyk, or equivalent) against all dependency manifests and container images. Generate SBOM in CycloneDX or SPDX format. Flag any critical or high-severity CVEs. Reject commits introducing new critical CVEs unless an exception ticket is linked. |
| **Human does** | Review flagged CVEs. Decide whether to: update the dependency to a patched version, accept the risk and document the exception, or change the design to remove the dependency. Sign off on exceptions with business justification. |

## Inputs

| Artifact | Source Control | Description |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| Source code repository (pom.xml, package.json, requirements.txt, Dockerfile, etc.) | All dependency manifests and container image configurations |  |
| CVE database (NVD, OSV, GitHub Advisory) | Current vulnerability intelligence |  |


## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| SCA scan report | report | Detailed list of all detected vulnerabilities with CVSS scores, affected components, and remediation guidance | 7 years (regulatory audit trail) |
| Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) | evidence | Machine-readable SBOM (CycloneDX or SPDX format) capturing all direct and transitive dependencies with versions, licences, and vulnerability status | 7 years (regulatory audit trail) |
| Exception tickets (if any) | decision | Documented exceptions for accepted vulnerabilities, including business justification and approval sign-off | 7 years |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_change |
| **SLA** | 15 min |
| **Tooling** | OWASP Dependency-Check, Trivy, Snyk, GitHub Advanced Security (Dependabot) |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Security Team Lead |

Critical or high-severity CVEs block commit unless an exception ticket is linked. If exception process cannot be completed in time, the commit is rejected and must be resubmitted with dependency updates.

## Dependencies

- `QC-01`
- `SC-08`


## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 25(1) | Testing must include "open source analyses" as a required methodology |
| DORA | Art. 28 | ICT third-party risk management requires identification and assessment of third-party components |
| DORA | Art. 8(4-5) | Identification and documentation of all ICT assets, including third-party dependencies |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(3) | Supply chain integrity — dependencies are supply chain components |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security · **Owner role:** Application Security Engineer
